★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.55 4.64 3.79 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.48 0.81 0.40 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.35 1.03 1.12 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.72 2.80 2.26 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.83 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.13 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-12-11 33 7 26 734 1 734
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-08-30 36 10 27 272 1 272
Total weighted health score
618.5
lower is better

Deficiencies (73)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

4 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
58 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
7 G/H/I Actual harm
4 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. L 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted E 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0710 Obtain a doctor's order to admit a resident and ensure the resident is under a doctor's care. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0712 Ensure that the resident and his/her doctor meet face-to-face at all required visits. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. F 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. F 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. F 2025-07-24 2025-08-22
